content.full_text:
August 9, 1976

Assemblyman Richard Hayden
State Capitol
Sacramento, California 95814

Dear Assemblyman Hayden:

Dan Lowenstein, Chairman of the Fair Political
Practices Commission, has asked me to respond to your
recent letter in which you indicated that you had
received a campaign contribution two months after it
was sent, because the contribution was mailed to an
incorrect box number. Obviously, you cannot
report a campaign contribution which was not received
on time even though the contributor may have reported
it on his or her own statement. We may wish to send
copies of the correspondence provided our office to the
Secretary of State's office to be included in your file
so that persons cross-checking the contributions will
understand the unusual circumstances which occurred.

If you have any further questions or comments, I
would be very happy to talk to you.

Sincerely,

Robert H. Stern
General Counsel

RHS:nc

REPLY TO:
□ SACRAMENTO OFFICES
STATE CAPITOL
SACRAMENTO, CALIFORNIA 95814
(916) 445-3532

□ DISTRICT OFFICE
20100 HEMLOCK AVENUE, SUITE 224
CUPERTINO, CALIFORNIA 95014
(408) 446-4114

COMMITTEES:
FINANCE, INSURANCE, AND
COMMERCE
RESOURCES, LAND USE AND
WATER AND MEANS

EDUCATION,
LICENSING AND VOCATIONAL
STANDARDS SUBCOMMITTEE
JOINT COMMITTEES:
STATE'S ECONOMY
REVISION OF THE ELECTIONS
CODE

Assembly
California Legislature
RICHARD D. HAYDEN
ASSEMBLYMAN, TWENTY-SECOND DISTRICT

July 23, 1976

Mr. Dan Lowenstein, Chairman
Fair Political Practices Commission
925 J. Street, Suite 522
P.O. Box 307
Sacramento, California 95814

Dear Dan:

Pursuant to our verbal conversation of some months ago
involving the possibility of checks from campaign contributors
which were never received or delayed, it appears that such has
happened to my campaign committee.

A complete copy of the correspondence is enclosed which
I believe to be self explanatory.

Sincerely,

RICHARD D. HAYDEN

RDH:nc
Encl:

Union Oil Company of California

Union Oil Corp., P.O. Box 7600, Los Angeles, California 90051
Telephone (213) 486-6446

UNION

H. P. (Hal) Shawlee                                July 19, 1976
Manager, Civic Affairs


                                    Citizens for Hayden
                                    P. O. Box 875
                                    Cupertino, California 95014


Gentlemen:

     On May 25, I wrote you enclosing a Union Oil Company of California
check in the amount of $200, payable to your committee, in support
of Assemblyman Richard D. Hayden's campaign for reelection to the
State Assembly, District 22. That original letter, envelope and
check are enclosed with this letter.

     You will note that my original letter was misaddressed to P.O. Box 575
instead of the correct address of P.O. Box 875. Apparently my original
letter was delivered to Julene Dee Pief who kept it for some time and
then on July 13 returned our letter and check to me along with a
covering letter. I am enclosing the Pief letter and envelope for you
also.

     This action was taken following my discussion with Mr. Logan of
Assemblyman Hayden's office. Since it originally was our intent to
indicate support for Mr. Hayden and since we reported the contribution
in our company's report as an "Employer of a Lobbyist" for the month
of May, it seemed that sending the check to you was most appropriate
action to be taken.

                                        Sincerely,


                                        [illegible]

aj

Enclosures

Union Oil Company of California

Union Oil Center, P.O. Box 7600, Los Angeles, California 90017
Telephone (213) 486-6446

union

H. P. (Hal) Shawlee                                May 25, 1976
Manager, Civic Affairs


                                        Citizens for Hayden
                                        P.O. Box 575
                                        Cupertino, California 95014


Gentlemen:

     Enclosed is a Union Oil Company of California check in the
amount of $200, payable to your committee in support of
Assemblyman Richard D. Hayden's campaign for re-election
for State Assembly, District 22.

     Our company will report this contribution on its next Major
Donor's Campaign Statement. You should record the contribu-
tion as being from "Union Oil Company of California, 461 South
Boylston Street, Los Angeles, California 90017 -- transmitted
by Harold P. Shawlee, Manager, Civic Affairs."

     Best wishes for a successful campaign.

                                        Sincerely,


                                        [illegible]

ANGII JUL.G

JUL 15 1976

H. P. SHAWLEE

JUL 15 1976


July 13, 1976


Harold P. Shawlee, Manager, Civic Affairs
Union Oil Company of California
Union Oil Center, P.O. BOX 7600
Los Angeles, California    90051

Dear Mr. Shawlee:

This letter came in our P.O. BOX, and we tried to foward
it to CITIZENS FOR HAYDEN, but were unable to find their
address.

I am returning it to you as maybe you have the correct address.

Sincerely,

[signature]

Julene Dee Pief
16950 Placer Oaks Rd.
Los Gates, California    95030


Harold P. Shawlee, Manager, Civic Affairs
Union Oil Company of California

Obviously, you cannot report a campaign contribution which was not received on time even though the contributor may have reported it on his campaign statement. You may wish to send copies of the correspondence provided our office to the Secretary of State's office to be included in your file so that persons cross-checking the contributions will understand the unusual circumstances which occurred. If you have any further questions or comments, I would be very happy to talk to you. Sincerely, Robert M.
